card.intuition: Zero-shot: classify/generate without any task examples — relies purely on pre-trained knowledge and instruction understanding. Few-shot (k-shot): provide k labelled examples in the prompt (in-context learning) — model infers the pattern. Meta-learning / Prototypical Networks: explicitly train for few-shot generalisation by learning from many episodes, each simulating a k-shot classification task.
card.analogy: A polyglot hired as a translator: zero-shot is asking them to translate a language they've never heard of based only on the language name; few-shot is showing them 3 example sentence pairs and letting them infer the pattern. The polyglot's broad language knowledge (pre-training) makes both possible.
card.time_complexity: In-context: O(T · d²L) per query (T = total context length); Prototypical: O(k · d) to compute prototypes
card.space_complexity: O(k · d) for k example embeddings at inference
when_to_use: Zero-shot: instruction-following tasks, zero annotation budget
Few-shot: when a handful of labelled examples are available and fine-tuning is too costly
Prototypical Networks: when examples change frequently (e.g., new product classes appear daily)
gotchas: In-context example order matters — performance can vary by up to 30% across orderings
Few-shot performance is sensitive to example quality — use diverse, representative examples
Long few-shot prompts increase cost and latency quadratically (attention is O(T²))

exercise.prompt: Implement prototypical network classification: classify by nearest class prototype.
hints: Classify by finding the class whose prototype has the smallest squared Euclidean distance to the query embedding.
exercise.test_code:
# 2 classes, 2D embeddings
support_embs = [[0.0,0.0],[0.1,0.1],[5.0,5.0],[4.9,5.1]]
support_lbls = ['cat','cat','dog','dog']

# Query near class 'cat'
q1 = [[0.2, 0.0]]
preds1 = prototypical_classify(support_embs, support_lbls, q1)
assert preds1 == ['cat'], f'Expected cat, got {preds1}'

# Query near class 'dog'
q2 = [[4.8, 5.0]]
preds2 = prototypical_classify(support_embs, support_lbls, q2)
assert preds2 == ['dog']

# Multiple queries
queries = [[0.0,0.0],[5.0,5.0]]
preds3 = prototypical_classify(support_embs, support_lbls, queries)
assert preds3 == ['cat','dog']
print('PASS')
